title: Google Ads Fundamentals
description: Learn how to capture high-intent traffic using the world's largest search engine. This course covers Search, Display, and Video campaigns, keyword bidding strategies, and Quality Score optimization.

seo_metadata.title: Google Ads Fundamentals Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Master Google Ads. Learn search campaigns, keyword match types, bidding strategies, and Quality Score optimization.

learning_outcomes: Set up a Google Ads account and navigate the dashboard
Create effective Search campaigns targeting high-intent keywords
Understand the Google Ads auction and how Quality Score affects Cost Per Click
Utilize keyword match types (Broad, Phrase, Exact) effectively
Write compelling ad copy that drives high Click-Through Rates

lessons.title: The Google Ads Ecosystem
lessons.content:
Search, Display, and Video

Google Ads allows you to place text ads on search results, banner ads on millions of websites (Display Network), and video ads on YouTube. We focus primarily on Search, which captures users actively looking for solutions.

lessons.title: The Ad Auction
lessons.content:
Ad Rank and Quality Score

Your position on the page is determined by Ad Rank (Bid x Quality Score). Quality Score is based on expected CTR, ad relevance, and landing page experience. A high Quality Score lowers your cost per click.

lessons.title: Keyword Match Types
lessons.content:
Controlling the Net

Broad Match casts a wide net. Phrase Match restricts ads to searches including the meaning of your keyword. Exact Match restricts it to the exact intent. Negative Keywords prevent your ad from showing for irrelevant searches.

lessons.title: Bidding Strategies
lessons.content:
Manual vs. Automated

You can set manual CPC (Cost Per Click) bids to control costs strictly, or use automated bidding (like Maximize Conversions or Target CPA) to let Google's machine learning optimize bids in real-time.

lessons.title: Writing Compelling Ads
lessons.content:
Responsive Search Ads (RSAs)

Google mixes and matches the headlines and descriptions you provide to find the best performing combination. Ensure your keywords are in the headlines and you have a strong Call to Action.

lessons.title: Ad Extensions (Assets)
lessons.content:
Taking Up Real Estate

Extensions add extra information to your ad (sitelinks, callouts, location). They make your ad physically larger on the search results page, which generally increases Click-Through Rate.

mcqs.question: Which type of Google Ads campaign places text ads on the search engine results page?

options.content: Display Network Campaign
Search Network Campaign
Video Campaign
Shopping Campaign

mcqs.question: In the Google Ads auction, what two factors primarily determine your Ad Rank?

options.content: Your Max Bid and your Quality Score
Your total budget and the time of day
The color of your ad and the font size
How long your company has been in business

mcqs.question: What is a benefit of having a high Quality Score?

options.content: It guarantees the top position forever
It can lower your Cost Per Click (CPC) and improve your ad position
It allows you to bypass Google's advertising policies
It gives you free clicks

mcqs.question: Which keyword match type offers the tightest control over which searches trigger your ad?

options.content: Broad Match
Phrase Match
Exact Match
Negative Match

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of adding Negative Keywords to a campaign?

options.content: To prevent your ad from showing for irrelevant search queries, saving money
To insult your competitors
To increase the cost of clicks for other advertisers
To delete keywords you no longer want to use

mcqs.question: What is a Responsive Search Ad (RSA)?

options.content: An ad that automatically answers customer questions in real time
An ad format where you provide multiple headlines and descriptions, and Google tests combinations to find the best performer
An ad that only shows on mobile phones
An ad that is triggered by voice search only

mcqs.question: What is 'Target CPA' (Cost Per Acquisition) bidding?

options.content: An automated strategy where Google sets bids to get as many conversions as possible at or below the target cost you set
A manual strategy where you pick the exact price of every click
A strategy to maximize video views
A strategy that guarantees you will never pay more than $1 per click

mcqs.question: Why should you use Ad Extensions (like sitelinks or callouts)?

options.content: Because they make the ad smaller and less annoying
Because they provide more information and take up more space on the results page, often increasing CTR
Because they are required to run a campaign
Because they hide your website URL

mcqs.question: If your ad has a high Click-Through Rate (CTR) but a very low Conversion Rate, what is the most likely problem?

options.content: Your bid is too low
Your ad copy is compelling, but your landing page experience is poor or doesn't match the ad's promise
Google's algorithm is broken
You are using too many negative keywords

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a factor in determining Quality Score?

options.content: Expected Click-Through Rate
Ad Relevance
Landing Page Experience
The Maximum CPC Bid
